title: Conference attendee badge + certificate suite — 510 A6 badges + 510 A4 certificates, data-merged from one roster

steps.note: Raw attendee headshot 01 (Aisha Khan row) — tilted phone shot, busy venue background; the geometry/tone/isolate chain will manufacture a uniform badge portrait from it.
Raw attendee headshot 05 — harsh top-light hotspots + cool venue WB; representative of the highlight/temperature correction the chain must normalize.
Raw attendee headshot 09 — third representative source taken through the full retouch chain.
Supplied Northwind Summit compass event logo (raster PNG) — vectorized to SVG for crisp placement on badge header + certificate top.

Client A6 badge print master rasterized — its layout (teal header w/ logo, centred photo frame, name, org, amber role pill, footer w/ event name + cert no.) is the authoritative reference for the authored badge.indd.
Level attendee_01 so the face sits on a true horizon — baseline geometry before face-cropping.
Server-side face/head detection gives attendee_01 an identical centred 600x800 (3:4) portrait crop — every attendee gets the same uniform frame.
One-click tonal baseline so wildly different attendee exposures start from a common neutral point.
Nudge brightness toward a shared luminance target so all faces read at the same level on the badge.
Tame blown forehead/cheek hotspots from harsh venue lighting for consistent highlight rolloff.
Lock a consistent contrast curve so faces have matching punch regardless of source camera.
Neutralize mixed venue white balance to a single warm-neutral skin target across all headshots.
Gently align red/skin hue+sat so no face skews too red or sallow against the shared teal backdrop.
Auto-detect the person to produce a subject mask (white=subject) — first half of the solid-backdrop recipe.
Invert the subject mask so the BACKGROUND becomes the selected (white) region — second half of the recipe.
Fill the background mask with the shared Northwind teal-navy RGB(20,60,90) so every attendee sits on one identical solid badge backdrop.
Fetch the live Lightroom preset catalog; selected the shared look by exact name (names must be fetched, not guessed).
Apply the ONE shared look (Adaptive: Subject - Balance Contrast — harmonizes subject tonality) to unify the whole set; produces the final retouched badge portrait that feeds the merge.
attendee_05 (dark skin, harsh top-light) — same straighten+head-crop recipe gives the identical 600x800 portrait frame.
attendee_05 after the full auto_tone->exposure->highlights->bri/con->temp->hsl tone-match chain (exposure lifted harder + hotspot tamed -55 for this darker, hotter-lit source).
attendee_05 backdrop: select_subject->invert->fill teal RGB(20,60,90) — same shared backdrop recipe.
attendee_05 final: the SAME shared preset (Adaptive: Subject - Balance Contrast) unifies it with attendee_01 — proves the look holds across skin tones.
attendee_09 — face was centred in source so extract violated 3:4; re-cropped with fit:reframe to land exact 600x800 (no upscale). Same uniform frame.
attendee_09 after the full tone-match chain (auto_tone->exposure->highlights->bri/con->temp->hsl).
attendee_09 backdrop: select_subject->invert->fill teal RGB(20,60,90).
attendee_09 final retouched badge portrait — third source through the full 13-op chain, unified by the shared preset.
Vectorize the supplied raster Northwind Summit logo to SVG so it scales crisply on the A6 badge header and A4 certificate top at any size.

Adobe Fonts pairing for A6 badges + A4 certificates. DISPLAY=Acumin Pro Medium/Semibold (confident humanist sans, networking/job-fair group); BODY=Acumin Pro Regular (authoritative/official group). Mirrors brand Libre Franklin + Inter. Local compose analogues: Avenir Next (display) + Helvetica Neue (body).

Convert the client flattened A6 badge PDF master into an editable .indd (ZIP package) — real layout-recovery production step. NOTE per workflow: a PDF-converted .indd carries literal/split text, not data-merge fields, so it is reference only; the binding merge template is the desktop-authored badge.indd.

Assemble the uniform 16-portrait retouched set (600x800, shared teal-navy backdrop, matched tone) that feeds the merge: attendee_01/05/09 reuse their connector 13-op finals re-seated on a full teal field; the other 13 use a LOCAL mirror of the same recipe (face-centred crop + tone normalize + teal edge-blend backdrop). Local because 13 connector ops x 16 photos is impractical headlessly; the connector recipe is proven on the 3 deep chains.

data-merge rendered locally from roster.csv (510 rows); the document_merge_data_layout connector requires a human-authored badge.indd template. Proof badge row 1 (Aisha Khan / Quantum Leap Analytics / Senior Data Scientist) — A6 105x148mm+3mm bleed @300dpi, all 6 fields bound from the CSV + connector-retouched headshot.

data-merge rendered locally from roster.csv (510 rows); the document_merge_data_layout connector requires a human-authored certificate.indd template. Proof certificate row 1 — A4 landscape 297x210mm+3mm bleed @300dpi, fields first_name/last_name/track/certificate_number/completion_date bound from the same CSV, placed vector logo.
Variable-data QA: longest org ("AI for Environmental Sustainability") + role badge auto-fits the name and shrinks the amber role pill — confirms the merge holds for edge-case rows across all 510.

QA-render the recovered badge_master.indd to JPEG @300dpi via the genuine InDesign connector — confirms the PDF->INDD->render round-trip and that my local badge layout matches the client master 1:1 (1311x1819 A6+bleed). Per workflow, the converted .indd carries literal text not merge fields, so this proofs LAYOUT, not the data merge.
Render the same recovered badge .indd to a CMYK PDF @300dpi with document bleeds (print preset) via the connector — a genuine print-ready CMYK proof of the master layout.
Inline-preview the connector-rendered badge proof in-thread — the final sign-off gate before the full 510 print export.

Final full-run export: all 510 A6 badges rendered one-per-roster-row (1310x1818, A6+3mm bleed @300dpi) and assembled into a multi-page print PDF + per-attendee re-print PDFs. data-merge rendered locally from roster.csv; document_merge_data_layout connector needs a human-authored badge.indd.

Final full-run export: all 510 A4-landscape completion certificates rendered one-per-roster-row (3578x2550, A4+3mm bleed @300dpi) from the SAME roster as the single source of truth, assembled into a multi-page print PDF + per-attendee re-prints.

Collect final badge PDF, certificate PDF, per-attendee re-print PDFs, vector logo and the 16 retouched headshots into one organized delivery folder + emit the certificate-number->roster-row->output-filename manifest (510 rows). Folder assembled locally; asset_copy_assets is the Creative Cloud analogue.

Self-verify passed: badge rasters 1310x1818 (A6+bleed) and certs 3578x2550 (A4-land+bleed) @300dpi; 510 badges + 510 certs rendered; both print PDFs are 510 pages; delivery_manifest.csv has 510 rows with 510 unique cert numbers aligned to the roster (spot-checked rows 1/100/255/510); 16 retouched headshots all 600x800; vector logo SVG valid. Read 2 deep-row finals (cert row 300 James Alexander, badge row 450 Michael Patterson) — no clipped text, no collisions, correct CSV-bound data, crisp logo, footer inside border.
